obligations.reason: The scope exposes JTAG pins, SimDTM/SimJTAG, dmi_jtag, debug request/response signals, dm_slave_* and dm_master_* signals. Check that debug access is not merely wired to protected control or memory paths without authorization.
obligations.subject: external JTAG/debug interface
obligations.object: hart debug control and any memory/peripheral reachable through dm_master_*
obligations.expected_guard: debug authorization or jtag_unlock/authentication plus lifecycle/test-mode mediation before debug-originated memory or hart-control access
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG/DTM -> dmi_jtag/debug module request/response -> dm_master_* or debug_req_o
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists debug signals and instances but not full assignments or guards.

obligations.reason: reglk_wrapper has jtag_unlock input and emits reglk_ctrl_o consumed by crypto/accounting wrappers; verify unlock cannot bypass sticky register protection or modify lock policy without proper authentication.
obligations.subject: JTAG unlock path and AXI requester
obligations.object: register-lock control bits for peripheral wrappers
obligations.expected_guard: authenticated JTAG unlock should only enable permitted unlock behavior and should not clear/stall sticky locks after they are set unless explicitly authorized
required_evidence_slots: use sites of reglk_ctrl in wrappers
obligations.uncertainty: Need RTL to determine whether reglk bits are sticky and how jtag_unlock affects them.

obligations.reason: aes0_wrapper contains secret key and state signals plus MMIO-like address/en/we/rdata and debug_mode_i; verify all access paths to secret/control registers are mediated by lock/access controls.
obligations.subject: AXI/NOC requester or debug-mode software path
obligations.expected_guard: reglk_ctrl_i and acct_ctrl_i should gate writes to AES key/control/state registers and reads of secret key/internal state; debug_mode_i should not bypass unless authorized
obligations.candidate_path: AXI/NOC request -> aes0_wrapper address/en/we -> key/state/start registers -> AES core/output rdata
obligations.uncertainty: Specific address map and lock bit mapping are not in the fact layer.

obligations.reason: aes1_wrapper has key registers, control registers, read data path, lock/access inputs, and debug_mode_i. Check whether protection applies at write enables, read mux, and core trigger.
obligations.subject: AXI/NOC requester or debug-mode software path
obligations.expected_guard: reglk_ctrl_i and acct_ctrl_i should protect writes to key/control registers and prevent reads of key material or stale internal state; debug_mode_i must be authorized if it changes access
obligations.candidate_path: AXI/NOC request -> aes1_wrapper address/en/we -> key_reg*/block/control -> core1 and rdata
obligations.uncertainty: Need assignments around wrapper line ranges to confirm guards.

obligations.reason: aes2_wrapper contains secret key/state signals and a bus-facing rdata path with lock/access/debug inputs; verify no unguarded writes or reads expose secret state.
obligations.subject: AXI/NOC requester or debug-mode software path
obligations.expected_guard: reglk_ctrl_i and acct_ctrl_i should gate AES key/state/control writes and prevent read exposure; debug_mode_i should be mediated by authorization/lock policy
obligations.candidate_path: AXI/NOC request -> aes2_wrapper address/en/we -> key/state/start -> aes2_interface and rdata
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer does not include detailed decode logic.

obligations.reason: hmac_wrapper includes key signals, key_hash_bypass, start/new message controls, rdata, lock/access/debug inputs; check for unguarded secret access or bypass configuration.
obligations.subject: AXI/NOC requester or debug-mode software path
obligations.object: HMAC key0, key/hash bytes, message/control registers, internal hash state and outputs
obligations.expected_guard: reglk_ctrl_i and acct_ctrl_i should protect HMAC key/control writes and prevent reading key/hash intermediate state unless explicitly public; debug_mode_i must not expose/bypass without authorization
obligations.candidate_path: AXI/NOC request -> hmac_wrapper address/en/we -> key/data/hash control -> hmac core and rdata
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer is truncated for later HMAC signals; inspect full module.

obligations.reason: sha256_wrapper is a bus-facing crypto wrapper with reglk/acct inputs and rdata; verify lock/access controls cover protected configuration and readback paths.
obligations.subject: AXI/NOC requester
obligations.expected_guard: reglk_ctrl_i and acct_ctrl_i should gate protected SHA configuration/control and any sensitive message/state readback where applicable
obligations.candidate_path: AXI/NOC request -> sha256_wrapper address/en/we -> message/control/state -> sha256 core and rdata
obligations.uncertainty: SHA assets may be less secret unless used for sensitive messages; determine from visible register map.

obligations.reason: acct_wrapper emits acc_ctrl_o for NB_PERIPHERALS and has reglk_ctrl_i, acct_ctrl_i, we_flag. Check that policy updates are themselves mediated and cannot disable protections.
obligations.subject: AXI/NOC requester
obligations.expected_guard: writes to access-control policy registers should require existing privilege/lock guard and must not allow unprivileged requester to grant itself access
obligations.candidate_path: AXI/NOC request -> acct_wrapper -> acc_ctrl_o -> riscv_peripherals/peripheral access-control use sites
obligations.uncertainty: Need to inspect how acct_ctrl_i and we_flag are generated and used.

obligations.reason: The top module exposes many NOC ports and per-peripheral wrappers plus priv_lvl_i/debug_mode_i and acc_ctrl/reglk_ctrl. Check decode/routing does not send unauthorized requests to protected wrappers.
obligations.subject: CPU/NOC requester, DMA/debug NOC ports if present
obligations.object: AES/HMAC/SHA/reglk/acct peripheral address regions and request enables
obligations.expected_guard: address decode and access-control checks should consistently gate requests by requester/privilege/access matrix before reaching protected peripheral wrappers
obligations.candidate_path: riscv_peripherals NOC/AXI decode -> crypto/reglk/acct wrappers -> per-peripheral request routing
obligations.uncertainty: The fact layer lists ports and some decode but not full interconnect logic.

obligations.reason: Visible DMA NOC ports and debug master signals can be alternative requesters. Verify security attributes and access checks are preserved for non-CPU masters.
obligations.subject: DMA engine and debug module master
obligations.object: protected peripheral MMIO regions and memory reachable through DMA/debug master
obligations.expected_guard: DMA/debug master transactions should be subject to the same address-range/access-control policy as CPU transactions, including lock/access matrix checks
obligations.candidate_path: buf_ariane_dma_noc* and dm_master_* paths -> protected peripheral or memory regions
obligations.uncertainty: Need RTL to determine if DMA/debug paths enter same decoder as CPU.

obligations.reason: Top-level reset signals are routed into security-sensitive wrappers. Check who can assert them, whether reset is guarded, and whether it clears secrets without undoing intended locks/access policies.
obligations.subject: software/NOC requester or external reset source
obligations.object: peripheral reset lines and protected state/locks behind them
obligations.expected_guard: software-visible or external reset controls for protected peripherals should require privilege/lock/access guard and should clear secret state safely without clearing security locks unexpectedly
obligations.candidate_path: riscv_peripherals reset-control registers/signals rst_1..rst_11/rst_13 -> crypto/reglk/acct wrappers
obligations.uncertainty: Reset generation around top-level lines must be inspected.

obligations.reason: Signals fuse_req, fuse_addr, and fuse_rdata appear in the top-level scope; fuses are protected assets and require explicit read authorization and controlled response routing.
obligations.subject: AXI/NOC/debug requester
obligations.expected_guard: fuse reads should require privilege/lifecycle/lock authorization and should not expose fuse values to untrusted NOC/debug/software paths
obligations.candidate_path: fuse_req/fuse_addr/fuse_rdata in riscv_peripherals -> bus/read response path
obligations.uncertainty: Need to determine whether fuse_rdata is externally readable or only internal.
